TRAITS
character with Good Reputation iS as well known to his adversaries a5 t0 his friends result, the character receives +3 TN Modifier on all Skill Checks that involve concealing his identity. such as Disguise_
beyond the scope of this book: If the players really want to por- tray landed nobles in MW3, the simp est way for the gamemas ter t0 deal with is t0 assume that the additional income gen- erated by the land holding is almost entirely offset by the addi- tional costs associated with its operation (such as hiring lack eys t0 the holding while the player character is off adven- turingi. general, landed nobles should never have to worry about any incidental expenses such as tood, ammunition tuel: They will also have permanent residence within the region_ usually mansion or ancestral castle They will have t0 deal with other expenses just like other characters_ addition to the economic benefits_ ruling noble assumed to be Well-Connected (p. 89) in the region he controls at no additional cost: However; rulers also attract rivals for their power, such that every Land Grant also carries with it 1-point Enemy who is considered t0 be rival to the throne. This trait not normally held by Clan characters since the Clans not manage their property feudal system: However; itis conceivable that Clan-born character could somehow hold title the Inner Sphere_ which case land grant might also be possible_
GOOD VISION Cost: 1 Tnis trait means the character' 5 vision iS better than usual; enough provide moditier vision-based Perception Checks. Note that this does not affect combat TNs
GREGARIOUS Cost: character with this trait iS outgoing and works well in social situations. Naturally. this iS great help in interpersonal dealings such that any target numbers where the gamemaster feels Inis ability would apply are reduced by
IN FOR LIFE Value Your character is (0r was) member of a large and secre tive organization such as the yakuza some other organized crime group or government agency: Although membership in such groups often provides benefits it is also said that you can never retire from them; you are in for life . Even if You are no longer actively working for the organization it can call on you for favors or even full-blown missions at any time. Clearly: this can cramp your style at the worst possible times is up to the gamemaster t0 decide when this trait comes into play; but he should not force the character t0 work constantly for the secret organization_ especially since the other characters in the group will probably be drawn into the missions as well;
LEMON Value: There always seems t0 be one vehicle in every unit that is cursed and this time it'$ yours. By reducing the Character Point cost Of your Vehicle Trait by 2 points you take on lemon Your vehicle will have technical problems at bad times and will generally be hard t0 maintain; repair and even reload The spe cific effects this has on your vehicle are Up to the gamemaster, although in any case all Action Checks for repairing and main- taining the vehicle have an additional +1 modifier.
INTROVERT Value: character with this trait is not very outgoing and has lim- ited insight into the feelings of others_ Naturally; this IS hin drance in interpersonal dealings such that any target numbers where (he gamemaster feels this trait would interfere are increased by
LOST LIMB (multiple troit) Value: 1/3/5 Either through birth defect Or injury; this character has ost all or part 0i one Of his limbs_ The value of this trait is based on the limb lost. Loss of one Or more fingers is 1-point trait; loss single hand or foot is 3 point trait: loss of single arm or leg is 5-point trait. This trait can be taken multiple times for multiple lost limbs_ The   specific effects vary   depending the limb lost; though due to the nature of this trait the gamemaster will often have t0 be the final arbiter on how restricts character based on the specific circumstances These effects assume the char- acter has no prosthetic replacement for the lost limb: for infor- mation on   prosthetic  replacement limbs see 144 of the Equipment section lost finger (or two or three results in reduction in manua dexterity. Any use of DEX-related skills or actions requir- ing fine use of the hands has +1 TN modifier. missing hand can be much more limiting: In general, apply +2t0 +4 Modifier to any DEX-related TNs, depending on the task; missing arm can de very limiting in certain situations making some tasks impossible and applying +2 to +4 Modifier t0 other tasks_
LAND GRANT Cost: Same as character' Title Land Grant added to Title Trait indicates that the charac ter not only holds noble title_ but that the title also gives him con: trol over region; This provides the character with tax income but administering the region becomes his responsibility (a responsi: bility most nobles delegate t0 hired lackeys). See 170 for more nobility and government in the BattleTech universe_ The cost of Land Grant Trait is based on the character 5 Title Trait: the cost i5 the same such that character with point Title must pay Character Points for his Land Grant. The size of the grant will be commensurate with the character" noble title_ The intricate workings of Iand holdings ad tax income are
